title: Shakshuka
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Ottolenghi-style shakshuka: spiced, saucy, with metric ingredients and exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
intro: Ottolenghi's shakshuka is the dish that made spiced tomato eggs a global obsession. This Chef Aly Thermomix version keeps the bold, warm spices while making the method simple, metric and practical for TM6 and TM7 home cooks.
ingredients: 1 red capsicum, deseeded and cut into chunks
Fresh parsley and crusty bread, to serve
steps: Place onion, garlic and capsicum in the mixing bowl. Chop 5 sec / speed 5. Scrape down.
Add olive oil, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika and chilli flakes. Cook 5 min / 120°C / speed 2 to soften and bloom the spices.
Add canned tomatoes, sugar and salt. Cook 10 min / 100°C / speed 2, with the simmering basket on the lid.
Transfer the sauce to a wide ovenproof dish or divide among 4 small skillets. Make 4 wells in the sauce with the back of a spoon.
Crack an egg into each well. Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 8–10 min until the whites are set but the yolks are still runny.
Scatter with fresh parsley and serve immediately with crusty bread.
tips: The sauce can be made entirely in the Thermomix, but the eggs need an oven — the sauce-to-oven method gives the best result.
For a richer sauce, add 50 g crumbled feta before baking.
Don't overcook the eggs — the runny yolk mixing into the sauce is the whole point.

faq.q: Can I make Shakshuka in a Thermomix TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The sauce is built in the Thermomix with exact times and temperatures, then transferred to an oven dish for the eggs. Both models work identically.

faq.q: Can I make the sauce ahead?
faq.a: Yes. The sauce keeps refrigerated for 3 days or frozen for 2 months. Reheat, make wells, add eggs and bake.
